description_long: This object consists of three traditional windmills arranged in a row on a rectangular patch of green terrain, resembling a grassy field. Each windmill features a cylindrical stone or brick base with a conical roof and four large, cross-shaped blades attached to a central hub. The blades are positioned in a horizontal-axis configuration, and the windmills are spaced evenly apart. The bases are textured to mimic masonry, while the roofs appear to be made of wood or shingles. The overall scene suggests a rural or park-like setting, with the windmills serving as both functional and decorative elements. The asset is visually cohesive, with natural colors and realistic proportions.
description: A row of three traditional windmills with stone bases and four-bladed rotors, set on a grassy rectangular terrain. The windmills are evenly spaced and feature conical roofs and cross-shaped blades.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three windmills aligned in a row, with the blades facing forward and the grassy terrain extending behind them.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the windmills from the left, highlighting the spacing between each windmill and the length of the terrain.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the backs of the windmills and the conical roofs, with the blades pointing away from the viewer.
description_view_3: Side view from the right, similar to view 1, emphasizing the even distribution of the windmills across the terrain.
